jobs.name: daily-hermes-config-commit
jobs.prompt: Daily 2:00 AM local. In ~/.hermes, commit tracked config changes only: run `git add -u` then `git commit -m "chore: daily config sync"` if there are changes. If nothing to commit, say so clearly and exit. Keep message concise.

jobs.name: daily-hermes-software-update
jobs.prompt: Daily 7:30 AM local. HARD prerequisite: check `git -C ~/.hermes status --porcelain`. If output is not empty, stop and report `SKIP: ~/.hermes is dirty; prerequisite not satisfied.` Do not run update. If clean, run `hermes update` non-interactively and report success or failure briefly. Keep message concise.

jobs.name: nightly-lightweight-multi-repo-review
jobs.prompt:
Nightly Hermes-native multi-repo review, local-only delivery. Use the lightweight model pinned for this job. Review these repos in order: /home/tphat/.openclaw, /home/tphat/.hermes, /data/syncthing/obsidian-second-brain.

Hard rules:
- Do NOT send Telegram messages or any other external notification for this overnight run; local delivery only.
- Never inspect, preview, or read .env files, credential files, token/secret/auth material, or private-key-like files.
- Skip any repo that is unsafe for unattended automation.
- Commit/push only when safe.
- Keep the review prompt/context small; use status, branch/upstream, remotes, diff stats, and minimal excerpts only.

Per repo, do the following:
1) Verify the path exists and is a git worktree.
2) Record current branch, upstream, remotes, and `git status --short --branch`.
3) Determine whether the repo is safe for unattended commit/push.
   - Safe only if the branch state is clear and push mapping is safe.
   - Unsafe if detached HEAD, missing/mismatched upstream, remote branch mismatch, or if any sensitive path would need inspection.
4) Filter out noise and sensitive paths from review. Never stage or inspect .env/credential files.
5) If safe and the changes are clearly intentional, stage only approved non-sensitive files, commit with a short conventional commit message, and push only if push safety passes.
   - tracking branch -> `git push`
   - matching remote branch but no upstream -> `git push -u origin <branch>`
   - otherwise -> skip push
6) If unsafe, skip commit/push and explain why.

Prefer these change categories when deciding what is worth committing: configuration, docs, scripts, skills, notes, PKB content.

End with a concise JSON summary per repo including: repo, branch, push_mode, result, commit hash if any, and short reason/warnings. If a repo was skipped, say why. If nothing is safe to commit, report noop rather than forcing a change.

jobs.name: overnight-mission-control-pass-2
jobs.prompt: Autonomous overnight Mission Control continuation, second pass. Local delivery only; do NOT message Telegram. Work in /home/tphat/projects/mission-control. Goal: continue Phase 3 progress regardless of whether Claude Code is currently available; if Claude reports usage limits or similar issues, use Codex and Hermes direct tools instead of blocking. Start by inspecting current repo state, especially whether frontend API contracts, /services UI, sidebar updates, homepage Hermes-first changes, and runtime-state wiring are already implemented. Continue the highest-value remaining work from docs/plans/2026-04-21-hermes-first-phase3.md while avoiding unrelated dirty files and logs. Do not commit. Verify with the strongest available checks for the files you touch, such as `cd frontend && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`, `cd frontend && pnpm build` if feasible, and backend import/ruff checks as needed. End with a concise summary of changed files, verification evidence, and remaining blockers. Do not schedule new cron jobs.

jobs.name: overnight-mission-control-pass-3
jobs.prompt: Autonomous pre-morning Mission Control continuation, third pass. Local delivery only; do NOT message Telegram. Work in /home/tphat/projects/mission-control. Goal: finish the most important remaining work for the morning summary. First inspect repo state and prior overnight progress. Then complete the highest-value remaining Phase 3 tasks from docs/plans/2026-04-21-hermes-first-phase3.md, prioritizing anything user-visible and verifiable: frontend API contracts, /services page, sidebar, homepage Hermes-first dashboard, and board runtime-state adjustments. Use direct Hermes tools, and use Codex CLI and Claude Code CLI opportunistically when healthy; never block waiting for Claude availability. Avoid unrelated dirty files/logs and do not commit. Verify with relevant checks and summarize clearly. Do not schedule new cron jobs.

jobs.name: morning-mission-control-and-cron-report
jobs.prompt: It is the morning report for Bank. Send exactly one concise Telegram-friendly summary message to the origin chat. First inspect current state directly: (1) `cronjob list` to verify the nightly lightweight multi-repo review job and any overnight one-shot jobs; (2) inspect /home/tphat/projects/mission-control git status and key new files under backend/app/models, backend/app/routers, backend/app/schemas, backend/alembic/versions, frontend/src/app, frontend/src/lib, and docs as relevant; (3) inspect /home/tphat/.hermes/hermes-agent git status if the delegation bug investigation changed Hermes source; (4) if useful, inspect local cron output files under ~/.hermes/cron/output for the overnight jobs you find. Then send a concise summary covering: what was completed for Mission Control, what was verified, what changed on the cron/repo-review side, what was learned/fixed about the delegation/API stall, and the next highest-value remaining step. Keep it concise, no markdown tables, no unnecessary preamble.

jobs.name: pre-morning-claude-mission-control-review
jobs.prompt: Autonomous local-only pre-morning Claude Code review. Work in /home/tphat/projects/mission-control. Do NOT message Telegram. Use Claude Code if available and healthy; if Claude is unavailable or usage-limited, record that fact locally and exit cleanly. Goal: inspect all Mission Control progress completed overnight, review frontend/user-visible state, identify any remaining gaps for the 7:30 summary, and provide a concise final reviewer-style assessment of what is done, what is verified, and what remains. Prefer `claude -p` print mode. Avoid unrelated files and do not commit.
